name: The Spring Thaw
description: Meltwater turns the yard to mud, but the fields drink deep and the first green shows on the hills.

choices.label: Sow early
choices.description: Gamble on the warm spell holding.

choices.label: Wait for firm ground
choices.description: Patience now, no lost seed later.

name: Midsummer Drought
description: Weeks without rain. The well sinks low and the crops stand brittle in the heat.

choices.label: Ration the water
choices.description: Thirsty work, but the crops survive.

choices.label: Dig a deeper well
choices.description: Timber and sweat against the dry.

name: Autumn Bounty
description: The harvest comes in heavy and gold. Carts groan toward the granary.

choices.label: Lay it all by
choices.description: Fill every store against the winter.

choices.label: Sell the surplus
choices.description: Trade fullness now for coin.

name: Founding Day
description: The anniversary of the first stone laid. The people look to you for a reason to celebrate.

choices.label: Throw open the stores
choices.description: A feast they'll remember through the dark.

choices.label: A solemn remembrance
choices.description: Honor the fallen; spend nothing.

name: The Salt Caravan
description: A salt trader's wagons creak up the road. Salt keeps meat through the winter — and he knows it.

choices.label: Trade timber for salt
choices.description: Preserve the larder against lean months.

choices.label: Trade stone for salt
choices.description: Stone you can spare; hunger you cannot.

choices.label: Wave him on
choices.description: Your stores will hold.

name: The Horse Fair
description: Drovers bring a string of rough ponies past the gate, good for hauling and, at need, for cavalry.

choices.label: Buy a team
choices.description: Hooves now save backs later.

choices.label: Let them pass
choices.description: The coin is needed elsewhere.

name: A Theft in the Stores
description: A sack of grain and a coil of good rope have walked off in the night. The quartermaster wants names.

choices.label: Hold a fair inquiry
choices.description: Justice seen is justice trusted.

choices.label: Flog a likely suspect
choices.description: Quick, harsh, and maybe wrong.

name: Two Families Feud
description: An old grudge between two households has come to blows in the yard. Both demand you take their side.

choices.label: Mediate patiently
choices.description: Hear both, favor neither.

choices.label: Bang their heads together
choices.description: Quiet now, resentment later.

name: Lightning Storm
description: A black squall walks across the hills, splitting the sky. Thatch smolders; the animals panic.

choices.label: Man the firebreaks
choices.description: Wet hides and bucket lines.

choices.label: Shelter and wait
choices.description: Let it pass; count the cost after.

name: Brawl in the Tavern
description: Ale and old grievances. The common room erupts, and a bench goes through a shutter.

choices.label: Wade in and break it up
choices.description: A few bruises, then peace.

choices.label: Let them tire themselves out
choices.description: Cheaper in blood, costlier in crockery.

name: A Clever Contraption
description: The workshop tinkers unveil a geared contraption they swear will save a hundred hours of labor — if it doesn't take a finger first.

choices.label: Fund the build
choices.description: Back their ingenuity.

choices.label: Too risky
choices.description: Hands are worth more than gears.

name: Sawpit Accident
description: A log slips its chocks at the lumberyard. There's a cry, then a terrible quiet.

choices.label: Rush the healers in
choices.description: Spare no effort to save the worker.

choices.label: Press on, shaken
choices.description: The wood must come in regardless.

name: A Pilgrim's Blessing
description: A pilgrim kneels long at your shrine and rises with tears, swearing the dark is thinner here than anywhere she has walked.

choices.label: Send her on with bread
choices.description: Her words will travel further than her feet.

name: The Tourney
description: The training yard proposes a day of contests — blunted blades, archery butts, and bragging rights for a year.

choices.label: Hold the tourney
choices.description: Sharpen pride and skill together.

choices.label: No time for games
choices.description: The walls won't man themselves.

name: Strangers on the Road
description: A ragged family stands at the gate, fleeing some trouble they won't name. They ask only for work and walls.

choices.label: Take them in
choices.description: Every hand counts when the dark comes.

choices.label: Turn them out
choices.description: You cannot feed every sorrow.

name: The Tax Man Cometh
description: A clerk of some distant crown demands levies for a war you've never heard of, waving a seal you can't read.

choices.label: Pay and be rid of him
choices.description: Coin is cheaper than soldiers at your gate.

choices.label: Question his authority
choices.description: Out here, your word is the only law.

name: A Cold Snap
description: Frost arrives weeks early and bites deep. Firewood burns twice as fast against it.

choices.label: Burn the woodpile
choices.description: Warm halls, lighter stores.

choices.label: Bundle up and endure
choices.description: Save the wood; spend the spirit.

name: A Vein of Iron
description: Diggers shoring up the granary footings strike a seam of iron ore — raw wealth, if you can work it.

choices.label: Work the seam
choices.description: Hard labor, hard metal.

choices.label: Brick it over
choices.description: The footings matter more than ore.
